items.q: Why did Durkheim see crime as functional?
items.correct: It reinforces collective values through punishment and reaction.
items.misconception: Because criminals provide a useful service.
items.why: Students miss that 'functional' means 'serves a purpose for social cohesion'. Crime clarifies moral boundaries when society reacts to it - the function is the REACTION, not the act.

items.q: What is the Marxist view on white-collar crime?
items.correct: It is widespread but under-policed because law serves ruling-class interests.
items.misconception: It does not really happen - only the working class commit crime.
items.random_wrong: It is treated more harshly than street crime.
items.why: Students misread Marxist theory as denying working-class crime exists. The Marxist point is about SELECTIVE ENFORCEMENT - all classes commit crime, but only some get prosecuted.

items.correct: A family formed when partners with children from previous relationships come together — a 'blended' or step-family — one of several family forms recognised in family diversity literature.
items.misconception: A reconstituted family is one that has been put back together after divorce.
items.random_wrong: A reconstituted family is a family living in temporary housing.
items.why: Reconstituted refers to the new structure of combining partners and children from different prior unions, not reuniting an original family. Students misread 'reconstituted' as restoration rather than reformation.

items.q: Weber's concept for empathetic understanding of social action is:
items.why: It's Verstehen. “Anomie” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which research method involves in-depth participant observation?
items.why: Correct answer: Ethnography. “Survey” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Durkheim publish 'Suicide'?
items.why: Exact answer: 1897 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
